headline: Test’s 1:1 had no substantive answers—this is a partial read driven by complete skips, not by any clear signal on wellbeing, engagement, clarity, or growth.
summary_bullets: Every planned check-in question was skipped, so there’s zero evidence on pace, friction, decisions, or what’s next.
The conversation produced no actionable content for priorities, blockers, communication, or manager support—silence is the only signal.
understanding_paragraph: Test did not provide any responses in this 1:1, so we learned nothing concrete about how the last two weeks felt or what’s blocking momentum. We also didn’t get their definition of quarter success or what they’d drop if work had to be cut in half. The only takeaway is that a normal feedback loop didn’t happen this cycle.

axes.meaning: No signal on energy or stress; score is weak and not actionable on its own.
No signal on ownership or motivation; score is weak and not actionable on its own.
No signal on priorities or expectations; we didn’t learn anything measurable here.
No signal on learning or stretch; we didn’t get any growth-direction input.

brutal_truth_employee: Test skipped every question in the transcript, including “How's the last two weeks actually felt — energy-wise?” and “What would make this quarter clearly successful for you — in one sentence?” This means there is no usable read on how Test is doing or what they need from you.
brutal_truth_manager: The conversation never recovered after the first skip (“What's been slower or harder than it should have been?”). Next time, when Test skips, the manager needs to immediately switch to a forced-choice or time-boxed prompt and explicitly confirm whether rescheduling is the real issue.

next_actions.action: Email Test a short, two-question async check-in: energy (1-5) and the single biggest blocker; ask for replies by end of day.
Schedule a 20-minute follow-up with Test and start with one forced-choice prompt: pick one—delivery stuck, priorities unclear, or energy low.
Re-run the close-the-loop step: ask Test to name one priority, one dependency, and one ask from you for the next two weeks.

watch_for: Before next 1:1: if Test skips again or answers in under 3 words, treat it as a broken feedback loop and rescope the format to async + forced-choice.
Within two weeks: if Test cannot name a single blocker or dependency, confirm whether they’re avoiding the conversation or blocked by missing context.
